steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage says Ezeulu "considered the immensity of his power" and then "wondered if it was real." The considering leads directly to deep reflection and doubt — the word must mean to think deeply or reflect on something.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. Pondered means thought about something carefully and at length. Substituting: "whenever Ezeulu pondered the immensity of his power...he wondered if it was real" — deep reflection leading to doubt fits exactly. A is correct.

B. Anticipated means looked forward to or expected something in the future. Ezeulu is not expecting his power — he is reflecting on power he already holds and doubting it. Eliminated.

C. Allowed means permitted or gave permission for something. Ezeulu does not allow his power to exist — he reflects on it. Eliminated.

D. Respected means held in high regard or admired. If Ezeulu respected his power, he would not question whether it was real. The text shows doubt, not admiration. Eliminated.
